properties.name: Chris McCandless's road, 1990–1992
properties.author: Jon Krakauer
properties.note: Generalized. He hitchhiked, so this is the shape of the journey rather than a turn-by-turn record. Approximate; for classroom use.

properties.name: The hand tram he never knew about
properties.note: A USGS gauging station downstream of his July crossing carried a steel cable and a hand-operated basket across the Teklanika. It appears on the topographic map of the quadrangle. He was carrying a road map of Alaska. Position approximate.

properties.name: Emory University, Atlanta
properties.region: The last day of the old life
properties.summary: Graduates with honors, gives his entire $24,292 savings to OXFAM, tells no one, and drives west. His family never hears from him again.
properties.q1_whats_where: Which direction does he drive, and what is the first major landform in his way?
properties.q2_why_there: Why does a person leave from a university rather than from home? What does a campus have in common with a trailhead?
properties.q3_relationship: What would you have to give away to leave, and what would you refuse to?

properties.name: Detrital Wash, Lake Mead
properties.summary: A flash flood drowns his Datsun in a dry desert wash. He strips the plates, burns his last $123, and becomes Alexander Supertramp.
properties.q1_whats_where: The wash has no water in it on the map. Where is the water that flooded it coming from?
properties.q2_why_there: Why does a dry desert channel become the most dangerous place in the landscape during a storm?
properties.q3_relationship: He burns his money on purpose. What is he trying to make impossible?

properties.name: Carthage, South Dakota
properties.summary: Works for Wayne Westerberg at a grain elevator. Well liked, hard-working, remembered fondly. The closest he comes to a home.
properties.q1_whats_where: What is the land doing around Carthage, and what is grown there?
properties.q2_why_there: Why is there a grain elevator in a town this small? What does a railroad have to do with where grain gets stored?
properties.q3_relationship: He is happy here and leaves anyway. Is there a place you left that you were happy in?

properties.name: Topock, Arizona
properties.summary: On an impulse he buys a secondhand aluminum canoe and sets out to paddle the Colorado to the Gulf of California.
properties.q1_whats_where: How many reservoirs does the Colorado pass through below Topock before it leaves the United States?
properties.q2_why_there: Why is the lower Colorado a chain of lakes rather than a river? What is all that water for?
properties.q3_relationship: What is the difference between spontaneity and recklessness — and can you tell which is which before the outcome?

properties.name: Golfo de Santa Clara, Mexico
properties.region: The river that does not arrive
properties.summary: He reaches the Gulf by way of the delta's maze of irrigation canals and mudflats, is towed out of a dead-end channel by duck hunters, nearly blown out to sea, and abandons the canoe.
properties.q1_whats_where: Find where the Colorado is supposed to meet the Gulf. Does the blue line actually connect?
properties.q2_why_there: Why doesn't the Colorado reach the sea most years? Follow the water upstream and name what takes it.
properties.q3_relationship: What else that you were taught exists turns out to be missing when you go and look?

properties.name: Bullhead City, Arizona
properties.summary: Works at a McDonald's, rents a room, opens a bank account. The most conventional stretch of his two years, and he hates it.
properties.q1_whats_where: What is on the other side of the river from Bullhead City, and why is that town there?
properties.q2_why_there: Why did a town this size have so many service jobs in 1991? Look directly across the Colorado.
properties.q3_relationship: He can live in the ordinary world perfectly well. He just doesn't want to. Does that make his choice stronger or weaker?

properties.name: Slab City, Niland, California
properties.region: A town with no town in it
properties.summary: Winters at the Slabs, the concrete foundations of an abandoned military base near the Salton Sea. Sells leather belts. Taken in by Jan Burres.
properties.q1_whats_where: Is the Salton Sea a lake? Where does its water come from, and where does it go?
properties.q2_why_there: Why does a settlement grow on an abandoned base with no water, no power, and no law? What does free land do?
properties.q3_relationship: He is beloved by nearly everyone on this road — and he will not call his parents. Hold both facts at once.

properties.name: Salton City — Ronald Franz
properties.region: The old man who wanted to adopt him
properties.summary: An eighty-year-old leatherworker who lost his own family asks to adopt him. The boy deflects and says they will talk when he gets back from Alaska. Franz drives him to the highway and watches him walk away.
properties.q1_whats_where: Find Salton City. Now find Fairbanks. Franz drove him to the start of that.
properties.q2_why_there: Why do so many of the people who help him live at the margins? What kind of place produces people who pick up hitchhikers?
properties.q3_relationship: An old man asks to be his family and he says not yet. What is he protecting, and what does it cost the people who love him?

properties.name: Carthage, Again
properties.summary: Returns to the grain elevator to earn money for Alaska. Westerberg thinks he is underprepared and says so. He leaves on April 15.
properties.q1_whats_where: Trace the road from Carthage to Fairbanks. How far, and through how many countries?
properties.q2_why_there: Why does the road north go through Canada? Look at the map and say why there is no other way to drive to Alaska.
properties.q3_relationship: Everyone who knows anything tells him he is not ready. When have you been warned and gone anyway?

properties.name: Fairbanks, Alaska
properties.summary: Buys ten pounds of rice, a used .22, rubber boots, paperbacks, and a cheap road map of the state. No compass. No topographic map.
properties.q1_whats_where: How far is Fairbanks from the bus, and in which direction?
properties.q2_why_there: Why is Fairbanks the last real town in that direction? Why is the road network beyond it so thin?
properties.q3_relationship: He buys food, a gun, and boots. He does not buy a map. What does the list tell you about what he thought the danger was?

properties.name: The Stampede Trail
properties.summary: Jim Gallien drives him out and grows alarmed: wrong boots, too little food, no real map. Gallien offers his own boots, offers to buy him gear, offers to come back. The boy refuses everything and walks into the snow.
properties.q1_whats_where: What is the last road he stands on, and where does that road go?
properties.q2_why_there: Why is it called the Stampede Trail? Who built it, and what were they hoping to reach?
properties.q3_relationship: A stranger offers him boots and he says no. What is he defending?

properties.name: Fairbanks Bus 142
properties.summary: About twenty miles in, an abandoned bus left decades earlier by a road crew as a shelter for hunters. He hunts, reads, shoots a moose and loses the meat to flies, and is happy for a long time. And he stays too long.
properties.q1_whats_where: How far is the bus from the trailhead, from the highway, and from the nearest cabin? Measure before you decide how far away he was.
properties.q2_why_there: Why was a bus out here at all? Who needed a road through this country, why did they give up, and what did they leave behind?
properties.q3_relationship: Twenty miles from a highway, and it may as well be the far side of the moon. What turns a distance into a barrier?

properties.name: The Teklanika River
properties.summary: He tries to walk out. The river he waded in April is now a grey torrent of snowmelt, waist-deep and fast. He writes that he is scared, turns around, and walks back to the bus.
properties.q1_whats_where: It is the same river he forded in April. What has changed between April and July?
properties.q2_why_there: Why is a northern river highest in midsummer? Where is the water in April, and what does the sun spend June doing to it?
properties.q3_relationship: He was not trapped by a wall or a storm. He was trapped by a season. What does it mean to be defeated by a calendar?

properties.name: The Hand Tram, One Mile Downstream
properties.region: What the map would have shown
properties.summary: THE POINT OF THIS MAP. Less than a mile downstream, a USGS gauging station carried a steel cable and a hand-operated basket across the Teklanika — built precisely so a person could cross when the river ran like this. It was on the topographic map. He had a road map. He walked back to the bus and died there about six weeks later.
properties.q1_whats_where: Measure the distance from the crossing to the tram. Now measure how long it would have taken to walk it.
properties.q2_why_there: Who put a cable across a river in the middle of nowhere, and what were they measuring? What does it tell you that the answer is the river's height?
properties.q3_relationship: He died, in part, because he did not have a map. Say what a map actually is, now, in your own words.
